patch.value:
## Context

ContextOptions (src/cli/commands/context.ts:34-53) has no parent field.
The context command registration in register-operations.ts (line 191+) adds no --parent option.
With 1000+ items, pm context --limit 10 still surfaces items from all epics mixed together.
Agents assigned to a specific epic would benefit from pm context --parent pm-mpbb as the 'what's happening in my scope' command.

## Acceptance criteria

- pm context --parent <id> scopes focus items and hierarchy section to descendants of that item
- Docs updated: AGENT_GUIDE.md and COMMANDS.md context section
- Shell completions updated for context command --parent flag
- CONTEXT_FLAG_CONTRACTS / MCP context tool parameters include --parent

## Evidence

- src/cli/commands/context.ts:34-53 (ContextOptions interface — no parent field)
- src/cli/register-operations.ts:191-234 (context command registration — no --parent option added)
- Context already computes childrenByParent (src/cli/commands/context.ts:473-487) so hierarchy grouping logic exists; parent scoping is a pre-filter step
pm context --parent <id>: scope snapshot to a single epic/parent subtree

pm context has no --parent or --epic flag to scope the focus-item and hierarchy sections to one epic or parent's subtree. Agents working inside a large Epic must either read the entire project context (noisy for 1000+ item repos) or manually run pm list-open --parent <id> + pm search to reconstruct what pm context would provide. A --parent <id> scope filter on pm context would make it the natural 'what's the status of this epic?' command.

- pm context --parent <id> scopes focus items and hierarchy section to descendants of that item
- Docs updated: AGENT_GUIDE.md and COMMANDS.md context section
- Shell completions updated for context command --parent flag
- CONTEXT_FLAG_CONTRACTS / MCP context tool parameters include --parent
